title: euphorbia missurica var. calcicola
intro: Euphorbia missurica var. calcicola, also known as the calcicolous spineless sandmat, is a plant species of the spurge family native to North America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its latex, which contains euphorbon and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and infections, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to promote healing and reduce pain. Herbal preparations of Euphorbia missurica var. calcicola can be made into salves, ointments, or infusions to utilize its medicinal properties, although caution should be exercised when handling the plant due to its toxic sap.
